hero.heading: ⏱️ Prometheus - Metrics Collection & Alerting
hero.subtitle: A reliable time-series database for monitoring, alerting, and scraping metrics from your services.
hero.whyLearn: Prometheus is the de-facto metrics store in cloud-native ecosystems and pairs well with Grafana for visualization.

storytelling.problem: You need to know application health over time: request rates, latency, error counts, but you don't have a stable place to store and query time-series data.
storytelling.solution: Prometheus scrapes metric endpoints, stores time-series, and provides a powerful query language (PromQL) to compute alerts and derive SLOs.
storytelling.analogy: Think of Prometheus as a ledger that records measurements over time so you can answer questions like 'what was the 95th percentile latency last week?'.
storytelling.realWorldExample: Prometheus scrapes exporters (node_exporter, cadvisor) and application metrics to monitor clusters and trigger alerts when anomalies are detected.
storytelling.deepDive: Prometheus uses a pull model (scraping HTTP endpoints), has a local TSDB optimized for fast ingest and queries, and supports federation for scaling.

architectureFlow.title: Instrument Your App
architectureFlow.description: Expose metrics via /metrics endpoint using client libraries
architectureFlow.details: Use Prometheus client libraries (Go, Python, Java) to export counters, gauges, and histograms.

architectureFlow.title: Scrape Metrics
architectureFlow.details: Jobs and targets in prometheus.yml define what to scrape and how often.

architectureFlow.title: Query & Alert
architectureFlow.description: Use PromQL to build alerts and dashboards
architectureFlow.details: Prometheus evaluates alerting rules and sends notifications to Alertmanager which routes to PagerDuty/Slack.

architectureFlow.title: Scale with Federation
architectureFlow.description: Use federation or remote write to scale and centralize long-term metrics
architectureFlow.details: Remote write allows storing long-term metrics in remote TSDBs like Cortex, Thanos.

beginner.description: Instrument code with counters, gauges, and histograms.
Learn queries: rate(), increase(), sum by()

intermediate.description: Create alerts and route them to notification channels.
Use histograms to compute accurate percentiles.

advanced.description: Strategies to reduce label cardinality and cost.

intro.what: Prometheus is an open-source systems monitoring and alerting toolkit that stores time-series data and provides a flexible query language.
intro.why: It offers fast, dimensional queries for metrics and a battle-tested alerting integration with Alertmanager.
intro.industry_importance: Prometheus is widely used to build SRE monitoring stacks and is a graduated CNCF project.

beginnerMistakes.title: High Cardinality Metrics
beginnerMistakes.description: Adding labels with unbounded values (user IDs) explodes storage and CPU.
beginnerMistakes.howToFix: Avoid high-cardinality labels, use aggregations or separate metrics.

productionSection.monitoring: Combine Prometheus with remote storage solutions (Thanos/Cortex) for long-term retention; use Alertmanager for dedup and routing.

labs.title: Instrument & Scrape
labs.description: Add a /metrics endpoint to a sample app and scrape it with Prometheus

steps.title: Add Client
steps.instruction: Add Prometheus client and expose metrics

labs.expectedOutput: Metrics appear in Prometheus UI and are queryable

interviewQuestions.question: What is PromQL and why is it useful?
interviewQuestions.answer: PromQL is Prometheus' query language for selecting and aggregating time-series; it's powerful for computing rates and percentiles over time.

promql_basics.description: Check whether scrape targets are healthy
Calculate request rate over five minutes
Estimate a latency percentile from histogram buckets

operational_tips: Scrape metrics from /metrics endpoints and keep label cardinality low
Use Alertmanager to route and deduplicate alerts
Store long-term data with Thanos, Cortex, or remote write backends
